records.title: Learn the basic rules for cycling in Dutch traffic
records.description: Cyclists must follow road signs, priority rules, traffic lights and designated cycle infrastructure while remaining visible and predictable around pedestrians and motor vehicles.
records.ai_summary: Use designated cycle paths where required, signal turns clearly and approach unfamiliar junctions defensively. Road design does not remove the cyclist's duty to obey signs, lights and priority markings.

records.title: Use legal bicycle lights and reflectors
records.description: In darkness or poor visibility a bicycle needs a white or yellow front light, red rear light and the prescribed reflectors, with restrictions on flashing and placement.

official_source.title: Rules for bicycle lights and reflectors

records.ai_summary: Test both lights before evening travel and keep spare removable lights available. Ensure reflectors are visible and lights face the correct direction without flashing or dazzling other road users.

records.title: Distinguish a legal e-bike from faster motor vehicles
records.description: A standard Dutch e-bike requires pedalling, has assistance limited to the legal speed and motor power, and follows ordinary bicycle rules; modified or faster vehicles can fall under other categories.

official_source.title: Rules for electric bicycles and fatbikes

records.ai_summary: Confirm the motor power, assistance cutoff and throttle behaviour before using an e-bike or fatbike on public roads. An altered vehicle may require registration, insurance, a licence and a helmet.

records.title: Wear an approved helmet on a speed-pedelec
records.description: A speed-pedelec is legally treated differently from an ordinary bicycle, and riders and passengers must use an approved helmet that carries the required certification.
records.ai_summary: Check that the helmet certification is accepted for a speed-pedelec and fasten it correctly before every trip. Also verify registration, insurance and road-position rules for the vehicle category.

records.title: Carry a young child safely on a bicycle
records.description: Children younger than eight carried on a bicycle need a safe seat with adequate support for the back, hands and feet, including protection against feet entering the spokes.
records.ai_summary: Use a correctly fitted seat suitable for the child's size, secure the harness and provide footrests with robust spoke protection. Recheck mounting points and balance before riding.

records.title: Do not cycle while impaired by alcohol
records.description: Cycling under the influence is prohibited and a cyclist who creates danger or obstruction can be prosecuted even though the consequences for a driving licence differ from motor-vehicle cases.
records.ai_summary: Choose walking, public transport or a sober rider after drinking. Alcohol reduces balance, judgement and reaction time, and bicycle status does not make impaired participation safe or lawful.

records.title: Report a stolen bicycle
records.description: The police advises checking whether a municipality removed the bicycle before filing a theft report and including the frame number so the bicycle can enter the theft register.
records.ai_summary: Keep the frame number, purchase evidence and clear photos separately. Check the municipal depot first, then report confirmed theft through the official police route and retain the report number.

records.title: Rent and return an OV-fiets
records.description: OV-fiets provides station-area bicycle rental using an eligible personal OV-chipkaart and subscription, with the rental ending only when the lock is closed correctly at return.
records.ai_summary: Confirm availability and eligibility before arrival, inspect the bicycle and open it with the registered card. Return it to the correct location, close the lock and verify that the rental ended.

records.title: Take a bicycle on an NS train
records.description: Non-folding bicycles generally need the current bicycle ticket and must follow time, space and train restrictions, while folded bicycles are treated under separate luggage conditions.

official_source.title: Bringing your bike on the train

records.ai_summary: Check the current time restrictions and buy the required bicycle product before boarding. Use designated spaces, follow staff instructions and consider OV-fiets when capacity is limited.

records.title: Use secure bicycle storage at railway stations
records.description: NS station bicycle facilities include guarded storage, self-service options, lockers and repair services, with local tariffs and access methods that vary by facility.

official_source.title: Bicycle storage at the train station

records.ai_summary: Check the specific station's opening hours, access method and free period before leaving a bicycle. Lock it, retain entry evidence and collect it before higher charges or local limits apply.
